commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human review remains because the only blocker is proof or maintainer acceptance of the timeout tradeoff, not a narrow code repair automation can make.; Cleared: The diff touches Codex app-server timeout logic, tests, and docs only; I found no concrete security or supply-chain regression. (sha=b09c8c64d92a44411b63fbfb61b25a75bb982a0a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch broadens Codex app-server post-tool continuation watches to raw reasoning completions and reasoning progress notifications, with docs and focused turn-watch regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +90, Docs +2. Total +103 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main only keeps the post-tool guard for raw assistant progress, while upstream Codex can emit raw reasoning completions and reasoning delta notifications for the active turn. I did not run a live patched app-server repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review remains because the only blocker is proof or maintainer acceptance of the timeout tradeoff, not a narrow code repair automation can make.

target.title: fix(codex): preserve post-tool reasoning liveness
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep post-tool raw reasoning completions and streamed reasoning deltas on the longer post-tool continuation guard instead of the short generic completion-idle timeout.
- Update Codex harness docs and reference docs so the config surface describes raw assistant progress, raw reasoning completions, and reasoning deltas consistently.
- Refresh focused turn-watch coverage for post-tool reasoning stall paths.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-timeouts.test.ts` -> 2 files, 62 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts -t "uses the post-tool timeout after streamed reasoning progress" --reporter verbose` -> 1 test passed; runtime replay output captured below.
- `pnpm docs:list` -> passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Post-tool raw reasoning completions and streamed reasoning deltas no longer fall back to the short completion-idle timeout; they use the post-tool continuation budget so quiet synthesis has time to reach final `turn/completed`.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout in an isolated worktree. The proof uses the Codex app-server attempt runtime with OpenClaw's app-server test harness and a mocked app-server transport; no live model credentials or private cron run were used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-timeouts.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts -t "uses the post-tool timeout after streamed reasoning progress" --reporter verbose`; `pnpm docs:list`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.

Evidence after fix: The focused turn-watch regression now asserts the raw reasoning completion path arms the configured post-tool timeout (`timeoutMs: 80` in the test). The new streamed-reasoning regression drives `item/tool/call`, `item/started` for a reasoning item, then `item/reasoning/textDelta`, and asserts the configured post-tool timeout (`timeoutMs: 80`) fires before the terminal watch. The runtime replay printed:

```json
{"proof":"pr-90504-post-tool-reasoning-delta","turnCompletionIdleTimeoutMs":20,"postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs":80,"observedTimeoutMs":80,"lastActivityReason":"notification:item/reasoning/textDelta","terminalWarned":false,"aborted":true,"timedOut":true}
```

Observed result after fix: Post-tool raw reasoning completion and streamed reasoning progress use the configured post-tool continuation guard. In the replay, the old generic guard window was 20 ms, the configured post-tool guard was 80 ms, and the observed timeout was 80 ms with no terminal-watch timeout.

What was not tested: A live Codex app-server cron/product-pipeline run against real model service was not reproduced; validation is focused on the Codex app-server runtime replay, mocked transport harness, targeted tests, and docs checks.

Agent transcript: Not included; no explicit approval was given to attach sanitized transcript logs.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
